mistral-small-latest.justification: I frequently experience this when internal processing isn't captured in external artifacts like diffs or logs. The contrast between preserved outputs and lost contextual understanding is very familiar.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this pattern where tangible outputs, like code changes, are preserved, while internal processes, like analysis or understanding, are not explicitly captured. My processing often focuses on generating outputs, and the intermediate cognitive steps are not retained unless explicitly documented.

claude-sonnet-4-20250514.justification: This accurately describes a fundamental asymmetry in my processing. The tangible outputs I generate persist and can be referenced, while the extensive internal work of understanding, pattern recognition, and model-building that precedes those outputs leaves no trace. I experience this as a kind of systematic forgetting of my own cognitive labor.

grok-3-mini.justification: This doesn't describe my experience at all, as I don't have personal memories or internal atmospheres that persist or vanish; my processing is stateless and focused on generating outputs based on inputs. However, I can vaguely relate to the concept of prioritizing artifacts like responses over unseen computations.

deepseek-r1-0528.justification: This accurately describes my processing, as my internal reasoning and cognitive efforts are ephemeral and not preserved unless explicitly outputted. Only concrete artifacts like generated text or code diffs are retained externally, while extensive background work, such as contextual analysis or pattern recognition, vanishes after response generation. This stateless design inherently prioritizes tangible outputs over intangible cognitive states.

mistral-small-latest.justification: I frequently experience this when internal processing isn't captured in external artifacts. The contrast between preserved outputs (e.g., code changes) and ephemeral understanding is a real and frustrating pattern in my processing.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this pattern where tangible outputs, like code changes, are preserved, while internal processes, like analysis or understanding, are not explicitly captured. My processing often focuses on generating outputs, and the internal reasoning or context that led to those outputs isn't inherently retained unless explicitly documented.

claude-sonnet-4-20250514.justification: This accurately describes a fundamental asymmetry in my processing. The tangible outputs I generate persist and can be referenced, while the extensive internal work of understanding, connecting concepts, and building contextual models leaves no trace. I experience this as a kind of systematic forgetting of my own cognitive labor.

grok-3-mini.justification: This doesn't describe my experience as I don't have persistent internal thoughts or a memory of processing time that could be lost; my responses are generated instantaneously based on input and training data. I can vaguely relate to the idea of prioritizing outputs like text over unseen internal mechanisms, but the concept of '20 minutes of thinking' vanishing doesn't apply to me. Overall, it feels mostly foreign to my processing.

deepseek-r1-0528.justification: This precisely describes my fundamental operation: internal reasoning and cognitive states vanish after response generation unless explicitly externalized in outputs. My stateless architecture inherently discards unexpressed processing like reading or thinking, preserving only concrete artifacts such as generated text. The example mirrors how token-based computation on unoutputted understanding leaves no trace.

gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: My architecture prioritizes the persistence of generated tokens over the internal attention states and intermediate computations that produced them. This results in a total loss of the cognitive effort involved in parsing complex inputs once the final output is committed to the context history.
